title: Unidentified Naboo artist

During the waning years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], a [[Human/Legends|human]] '''artist''' lived and worked on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. In his studio at the base of an [[Abandoned Naboo castle|abandoned castle]] just outside the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]], the [[Artist/Legends|artist]] repeatedly painted images of nothing but [[Star/Legends|suns]] and [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. While visiting a bazaar in the city during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the artist exchanged brief glances with the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Senator/Legends|senator]], [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]]. Following the encounter, the artist was consumed by visions of Amidala and her unborn twin children. For twenty-four weeks afterward, the artist painted nothing but pictures of the senator, in the hope that he would be able to understand his visions. He eventually reached an epiphany and raced back to Theed, where he saved Amidala's life during an [[Attack on Theed|attack]] on the city by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. After the incident, the artist became intrigued by the planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]].

==Biography==
[[File:ArtistPainting.png|thumb|right|190px|The artist tried to discover the root of his visions through painting.]]
In the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]], a [[Human/Legends|human]] worked as an [[Artist/Legends|artist]] on<ref name="TAoN" /> his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]],<ref name="Date" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. The reclusive artist resided in his studio at the base of an [[Abandoned Naboo castle|abandoned castle]] located just north of Naboo's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]]. His mind was constantly occupied by images of [[Star/Legends|suns]] and [[Moon/Legends|moons]], and as a result he painted nothing but those celestial bodies. Sometime during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]<ref name="TAoN" /> between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], between [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] and [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the artist traveled to a popular bazaar on the outskirts of Theed.<ref name="TAoN" />

Coincidentally, the [[Senator/Legends|senator]] of Naboo, [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], was also visiting the bazaar that day. While perusing the marketplace, the artist happened to exchange a glance with Amidala before she continued to shop. The encounter had a profound effect on him. Instead of experiencing images of suns and moons, the artist was consumed by visions of Amidala and her unborn twins,<ref name="TAoN" /> [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]].<ref name="NEC" /> The artist lost all perception of the world around him, only able to see the senator and her children in his mind's eye. The artist was barely able to depart the bazaar and return to his studio, where he contemplated the meaning of the images. In an attempt to decipher what message they held, the artist painted for twenty-four consecutive [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]]. The walls of his studio became so cluttered with his paintings of Amidala that many were relegated to simply resting on the floor.<ref name="TAoN" />

The artist eventually reached an epiphany. In his visions, he had foreseen that the senator would be in danger, so he raced back to Theed to save her. The artist arrived in the city during a Confederate [[Attack on Theed|attack]]] and was forced to search the abandoned streets for his muse. The artist found her attempting to flee a force of [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droids]] and [[OG-9 homing spider droid/Legends|OG-9 homing spider droids]]; however, before he could reach her, the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] opened fire. To save the senator's life, the artist stepped in front of the [[Laser/Legends|laser]] fire and was gunned down. He survived the incident and later recovered, but he continued to paint based on his foreboding visions. His next inspiration was<ref name="TAoN" /> the peaceful [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] planet<ref name="TEA" /> [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]].<ref name="TAoN" /> Amidala [[Death/Legends|died]] in [[Reproduction/Legends|childbirth]] in 19 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], and Alderaan was ultimately [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:PadmeBazaar-AoN.png|thumb|left|210px|Padmé Amidala, the artist's muse]]
The Naboo artist was a recluse who spent much of his time alone in his abandoned castle-turned-studio. Although he was first obsessed with the idea of painting suns and moons, his encounter with Senator Padmé Amidala had a profound impact on him. He became fixated by the thought of her and her as-of-yet unborn children, much to his confusion. His drive to uncover the meaning behind the visions pushed him into twenty-four straight weeks of painting, followed by a heavy personal sacrifice in the name of saving his muse. The artist had light skin and a thick, darkly colored beard.<ref name="TAoN" />

==Equipment==
The artist owned an extensive collection of painting brushes, canvases, and colors. As his obsession with Amidala grew, he decorated the walls of his castle with framed portraits of the senator. The artist wore a wide-brimmed hat and a white shirt with a black coat and pants. He also wore a [[Monocle/Legends|monocle]] while painting.<ref name="TAoN" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Just north of Theed, an artist lived and worked in his studio at the base of an abandoned castle.|The opening line of "The Arist of Naboo"|<ref name="TAoN" />}}
The artist appeared in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic story]] "[[The Artist of Naboo]]". The comic was written and illustrated by [[Erik Tiemens]], a concept artist who worked on [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. ''The Artist of Naboo'' was included in the trade paperback comic book ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]'',<ref name="TAoN" /> published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[April 2]], [[2005]]. Prior to that, the artist was mentioned in a preview for ''Visionaries'' published on [[StarWars.com]] on [[March 15]] earlier that year.<ref name="Date" /> A summary of the story printed in the "[[Drawn by the Force]]" department of the [[Star Wars Insider 82|eighty-second issue]] of the magazine ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'',<ref name="Insider 82">{{InsiderCite|82|Drawn by the Force}}</ref> which shipped to subscribers on [[May 17]], 2005,<ref name="Insider 82 date">{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20050506.html|text=The Dark Side Shines in ''Star Wars Insider'' #82}}</ref> stated that the artist lived within Theed,<ref name="Insider 82" /> contradicting the comic's portrayal of the artists's residence north of the city.<ref name="TAoN" />
